title: Thinking Machines releases Inkling, a 975B open-weights multimodal model
friendlyTitle: Thinking Machines releases a large open AI model called Inkling
summary: Thinking Machines Lab has released Inkling’s weights: a mixture-of-experts model with 975B total parameters and 41B active parameters, supporting text, image and audio input with up to a one-million-token context window. The company positions it as a customizable foundation model and offers fine-tuning through Tinker.
friendlySummary: Thinking Machines released Inkling, an AI model that can work with text, pictures and audio. People can download its model weights and tune it for their own jobs.
whyItMatters: A large open-weights multimodal model broadens the options for teams that need domain adaptation, deployment flexibility or control over their model stack. The company explicitly frames it as a balanced base model rather than claiming it is the strongest model overall.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This gives builders more choice when they want to adapt AI to a special task instead of using one general cloud model.
